NN.Runtime.External.Julia

source

Imports
  • Init
  • Lean
  • Lean.Data.Json
  • NN.Runtime.External.Process
Imported by
    Runtime.External.Julia.resolveJuliaCmd
    Runtime.External.Julia.isAvailable
    Runtime.External.Julia.ensureAvailable
    Runtime.External.Julia.run
    Runtime.External.Julia.runJson

    Julia subprocess integration (optional) #

    This module provides a small wrapper around IO.Process for calling out to Julia.

    Why this exists:

    • TorchLean sometimes treats external tools as untrusted producers (e.g. Julia doing a heavy numeric search on CPU/GPU) and then checks a small certificate artifact in Lean.
    • The IO boundary is explicit: any result returned by Julia should be treated as untrusted unless validated in Lean.

    This mirrors the existing patterns used in TorchLean for:

    • Arb via Python (NN/Floats/Arb/Oracle.lean), and
    • Gymnasium via Python (NN/Runtime/RL/Gymnasium/Client.lean).

    Optional dependency:

    • This file compiles without Julia installed.
    • Julia is only required if you execute code that calls run/runJson.
    • You can override the Julia executable path via the TORCHLEAN_JULIA environment variable.

    References:

    • Lean IO.Process: https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/Init/System/IO.html
    • Julia language: https://julialang.org/

    Resolving the Julia executable #

    source
    🔸 coverage
    def Runtime.External.Julia.resolveJuliaCmd (juliaCmd : String := "julia") :
    IO String

    Resolve which Julia executable to use.

    If the environment variable TORCHLEAN_JULIA is set, it takes precedence. Otherwise we fall back to juliaCmd (default: "julia"), which is expected to be on PATH.

    Equations
    • Runtime.External.Julia.resolveJuliaCmd juliaCmd = Runtime.External.Process.resolveCmdFromEnv "TORCHLEAN_JULIA" juliaCmd
    Instances For

      Availability checks #

      source
      🔸 coverage
      def Runtime.External.Julia.isAvailable (juliaCmd : String := "julia") :
      IO Bool

      Check whether Julia is available by running julia --version.

      This is conservative by design: any exception (including “executable not found”) is treated as “not available”.

      Equations
      • Runtime.External.Julia.isAvailable juliaCmd = do let cmd ← Runtime.External.Julia.resolveJuliaCmd juliaCmd Runtime.External.Process.isCmdAvailable cmd
      Instances For
        source
        🔸 coverage
        def Runtime.External.Julia.ensureAvailable (juliaCmd : String := "julia") :
        IO String

        Require Julia to be available and return the resolved command.

        This is suitable for example runners that want a friendly error message when Julia is missing.

        Equations
        • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
        Instances For

          Running Julia #

          source
          🔸 coverage
          def Runtime.External.Julia.run (args : Array String) (cwd : Option String := some ".") (juliaCmd : String := "julia") :
          IO String

          Run Julia with the given CLI arguments and return stdout.

          On nonzero exit code, we raise IO.userError including the captured stderr. The cwd defaults to "." so relative script paths behave like in other TorchLean subprocess integrations.

          Equations
          • Runtime.External.Julia.run args cwd juliaCmd = do let cmd ← Runtime.External.Julia.ensureAvailable juliaCmd Runtime.External.Process.runStdoutChecked "Julia" cmd args cwd
          Instances For
            source
            🔸 coverage
            def Runtime.External.Julia.runJson (args : Array String) (cwd : Option String := some ".") (juliaCmd : String := "julia") :
            IO Lean.Json

            Run Julia and parse stdout as JSON.

            Certificate producer scripts commonly print one JSON payload to stdout; this entrypoint checks the Julia process and parses that payload.

            Equations
            • Runtime.External.Julia.runJson args cwd juliaCmd = do let cmd ← Runtime.External.Julia.ensureAvailable juliaCmd Runtime.External.Process.runJsonStdoutChecked "Julia" cmd args cwd
            Instances For
